weeks.title: Week 3
weeks.introduction: This week focuses on strengthening tongue suction and control with new exercises.
weeks.overview: Continue building on the foundation from weeks 1-2 with more advanced tongue exercises.

exercises.title: Lingual Palatal Suction
exercises.instructions:
Keep your lips back in a smile. Open mouth as wide as possible without floor of mouth lifting. Use strong suction to hold the tongue to the roof of mouth and hold for as long as possible (Start with 10 seconds and work up to 1 minute). Try focusing on lifting the back portion of the tongue to the palate. If tongue is overflowing you can slowly open and close teeth together to help tongue squish inside the arch. Use provided chopsticks to push tongue inside of arch can also help.

**This exercise is most important to master prior to frenectomy**
exercises.compensations:
Watch in mirror for facial grimace (chin strain/dimpling), neck engagement, jaw protrusion and jaw lateralization, floor of mouth activation – use bite block to avoid

Watch for tongue overflow over teeth. This is an important metric for tongue tie surgery

exercises.title: Tongue Points (Left and Right)
exercises.instructions: With a neutral face open half way. Using the smallest point of the tip of the tongue, touch the smallest surface at the corner of the mouth. Repeat for 2 minutes. Keep your lips back in a smile and open half way. Do the same tongue movements for another minute. Tongue movements should be slow and controlled without dragging the tongue along the lips.
exercises.compensations:
Watch in mirror for jaw lateralization and jaw protrusion – use bite block to avoid

Continue with nasal breathing (Consider doing the unblocking your nose exercises first)

exercises.title: Cheek Puffs
exercises.instructions: Fill your mouth with air. Move air into the right cheek and hold for 5 seconds. Move air into the left cheek and hold for 5 seconds. Move air into the top lip and hold for 5 seconds. Move air into the lower lip and hold for 5 seconds. Repeat for 2 minutes
exercises.compensations:
Watch in the mirror for jaw lateralization and jaw protrusion. Keep teeth gently together to help avoid.

Continue with nasal breathing (Consider doing the unblocking your nose exercises first)

exercises.title: Belly/Diaphragm Breathing
exercises.instructions:
1. Lie on the floor face up with your knees slightly bent.
2. Place one hand lightly on your stomach and the other hand on your chest.
3. As you breathe, feel which hand is moving more, then begin to concentrate on breathing using your diaphragm (your belly), and not using your chest. Feel your stomach rise as your lungs fill with air from the bottom.
4. Let your stomach fall naturally when breathing out by relaxing your diaphragm.
5. Try placing a small object on your stomach, such as a small book or your cell phone, so that you can see the movement more easily and keep your breathing focused on that area.
6. Next, try this exercise standing or sitting up. Place your hands in the same position and focus your breathing the same way so that your stomach moves rather than your chest. This may take a bit more concentration.
7. Practice for 5 minutes at a time. Ideally, it's good to do this exercise first thing in the morning or at night before falling asleep because you are already in bed.

**Consider doing the unblocking your nose exercises first**

exercises.title: Middle of Tongue – One Elastic Hold
exercises.instructions:
Place elastic on the middle of the tongue (See Photo C) and hold it to the roof of the mouth.

If concerned about swallowing, tie a piece of floss to elastic to keep between lips.

weeks.title: Week 4
weeks.introduction: Continue practicing the same exercises from Week 3 to build consistency and strength.
weeks.overview: Maintain the same exercise routine to solidify muscle memory and improve technique.
